preparation_method: seasoned with garlic and oyster sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Tender Jade Cabbage is approximately 200 g, representing a shared vegetable side dish for one to two persons.

nutrient_source.title: Stir-fried Chinese cabbage (napa cabbage with oyster sauce and garlic) — adapted from USDA FoodData Central: Chinese cabbage (pe-tsai), cooked, boiled, drained, without salt (FDC ID 169975) with adjustment for oil and oyster sauce addition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base values derived from USDA FDC ID 169975 (Chinese cabbage, pe-tsai, cooked). Macros adjusted upward to account for stir-frying in vegetable oil (~5 g oil per 100 g finished dish) and oyster sauce seasoning (~10 g per 100 g finished dish), which increases energy, fat, sodium, and carbohydrate. This is a standard restaurant-style preparation. Protein and fibre values are consistent with cooked napa cabbage. Sodium elevated due to oyster sauce and soy sauce seasoning typical of this dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of non-starchy vegetables including Chinese cabbage — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked non-starchy vegetables and peer-reviewed literature on brassica vegetables
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tender Jade Cabbage (翠玉白菜). GI estimated at 15 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked non-starchy brassica vegetables (e.g., cooked cabbage GI ~10–20, low category). Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (15 × 11.6) / 100 = 1.74, rounded to 1.7. This is consistent with the very low carbohydrate content of the dish.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from oyster sauce and soy sauce seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 200 g restaurant serving of Tender Jade Cabbage contributes approximately 840 mg sodium, representing a significant portion of the 2000 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board; requesting reduced-sauce preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tender Jade Cabbage has a low glycemic index (estimated ~15) and a glycemic load of approximately 1.7 per serving, making it a suitable vegetable choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes as part of a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, high vegetable content, but moderate sodium from seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tender Jade Cabbage is low in saturated fat and provides beneficial dietary fibre and antioxidants from napa cabbage; however, individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium intake from the oyster sauce and soy sauce used in preparation and consider requesting a lighter seasoning.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tender Jade Cabbage (翠玉白菜, Cuì Yù Báicài) is a classic Chinese vegetable dish made primarily from napa cabbage (Chinese cabbage, pe-tsai) that is stir-fried or lightly braised with garlic, oyster sauce, light soy sauce, and vegetable oil. The dish is a staple side vegetable in Chinese restaurants across Singapore and mainland China, prized for its tender texture, mild sweetness, and glossy sauce coating. The cabbage leaves are typically cut into large pieces and cooked briefly over high heat to retain their crisp-tender bite and vibrant pale-green colour. The name 翠玉 (Cuì Yù) means 'jade green', referring to the translucent jade-like appearance of the cooked cabbage leaves. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories (approximately 62 kcal per 100 g), low in fat, and provides dietary fibre, vitamin C, folate, and potassium from the cabbage. The primary nutritional concern is sodium, which is elevated due to oyster sauce and soy sauce seasoning, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is very low (estimated ~15), making it an excellent vegetable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. In Singapore, this dish is commonly ordered as a shared vegetable side at zi char (煮炒) stalls and Chinese restaurants, often served alongside rice and protein dishes. It is not suitable for vegans or strict vegetarians due to the use of oyster sauce, and it contains soy and shellfish allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact match for Tender Jade Cabbage (翠玉白菜) as a prepared dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC ID 169975 (Chinese cabbage, pe-tsai, cooked) with adjustments for stir-frying oil and oyster sauce, representing a standard restaurant preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked non-starchy brassica vegetables. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC ID 169975 (Chinese cabbage pe-tsai cooked boiled drained without salt) with upward adjustment for approximately 5 g vegetable oil and 10 g oyster sauce per 100 g finished dish, consistent with standard Chinese restaurant stir-fry preparation. 2. Sodium value of 420 mg per 100 g reflects typical oyster sauce and soy sauce seasoning; actual sodium may vary by restaurant. 3. GI is estimated at 15 based on analogous cooked non-starchy brassica vegetables from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based vegetable dish containing no animal products that contribute B12; confirmed zero for the cabbage base. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as cooked napa cabbage contains no meaningful vitamin D; confirmed zero per USDA FDC source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as vegetable oil used in stir-frying contains negligible trans fats; confirmed zero. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish with no cholesterol-containing ingredients; confirmed zero. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FDC ID 169975 with proportional adjustment for the oil addition. 6. The dish is not halal-certified as oyster sauce may contain shellfish and soy sauce may vary by brand; halal status depends on specific ingredients used by the restaurant. 7. Allergens include soy (from oyster sauce and soy sauce) and shellfish (from oyster sauce). 8. This record was generated from a field survey data seed record for food_id GD000862 served by See Ya Tonight 今晚有局 restaurant.
